full_text:
- Mercury Crosses 40°C In Rajasthan | Snowfall Hits J&K, Uttarakhand, Himachal Pradesh
IMD issues heatwave alert in MP:Mercury crosses 40°C in Rajasthan: Snowfall hits J&K, Uttarakhand and Himachal Pradesh
New Delhi/Srinagar/Dehradun/Jaipur4 hours ago
Snowfall has returned to several hill states, bringing down temperatures in Jammu and Kashmir, Himachal Pradesh and Uttarakhand. Alerts for rain along with snowfall have also been issued in these regions.
In Himachal Pradesh, the temperature dropped by 8°C within 24 hours. Manali recorded 12.6°C, a fall of about 7°C. However, a heatwave alert has been issued in Solan today.
Snowfall was reported in Rudraprayag, Chamoli and Pithoragarh districts of Uttarakhand. An alert for rain and snowfall has been issued in five districts today. In Dehradun, the maximum temperature reached 34.1°C, and residents are feeling the heat.
In Rajasthan, maximum temperatures in Jaisalmer, Barmer and Balotra ranged between 39°C and 41°C, with Barmer recording the highest at 39.4°C. An alert for dust storms and rain has been issued in eight districts of the state.
A heatwave alert has also been issued in Madhya Pradesh from today. The maximum daytime temperature in Narmadapuram was 40.2°C, while temperatures crossed 38°C in 10 cities. Rain is likely in the state on March 15 and 16.
Pictures of the weather
Snowfall occurred at the North Portal of Atal Tunnel Rohtang on Thursday.
People are facing considerable trouble due to intense sunlight in Jaipur, Rajasthan.
Snowfall occurred in Lahaul Spiti, Himachal, from morning till evening on Thursday.
Fog covered Deoria, UP in the morning. Visibility here was less than 50 meters.
Weather for the next two days
March 14: The weather will remain dry and warm in Delhi and Uttar Pradesh. Rain or thunderstorms are likely in West Bengal and the North-East.
March 15: Temperatures are expected to rise in most parts of Delhi. Snowfall is likely in the hill states, while rain has been forecast in the North-Eastern states.
Weather across states
Madhya Pradesh: Day temperature hits 40.2°C, crosses 38°C in 10 cities
A heatwave has begun in Madhya Pradesh in the second week of March itself. On Thursday, heatwave conditions were reported in Narmadapuram, Ratlam and Dhar. A heatwave alert has also been issued for these areas today.
In Narmadapuram, the maximum daytime temperature reached 40.2°C, while temperatures crossed 38°C in 10 cities. Rain is likely in parts of the state on March 15 and 16.
Uttar Pradesh: Rain alert from March 15, dense fog in 5 districts
Fog has covered more than 10 districts in Uttar Pradesh. A new Western Disturbance is expected to become active from March 15, raising the possibility of rain in several parts of the state.
According to the Meteorological Department, this could be the season’s first rainfall and may bring down temperatures that have remained above normal for the past few days.
Rajasthan: Cloud cover brings relief from heatwave; milder temperatures likely for the next two weeks
The weather in Rajasthan changed from Thursday as cloud cover brought relief from the heatwave and intense heat. Clouds covered districts across the Jaipur, Ajmer, Bharatpur and Bikaner–Jodhpur divisions.
According to the Meteorological Department, the state is likely to see relief from extreme heat for the next two weeks.
Uttarakhand: Snowfall in Pithoragarh and Chamoli; rain likely in five districts
Snowfall has been reported in Chamoli and Pithoragarh districts of Uttarakhand. There is a possibility of rain and snowfall today in Uttarkashi, Rudraprayag, Chamoli, Bageshwar and Pithoragarh.
During the last 24 hours, maximum temperatures in both the hilly and plain areas of the state were recorded above normal.
Haryana: Day temperature drops by 0.3C, still about 5C above normal
An average drop of 0.3C was recorded in Haryana, although the temperature still remains about 5C above normal. According to the Meteorological Department, there is a possibility of rain in several districts of the state from March 14. On Thursday, the highest temperature in the state was 36.7C at Palwal Water Service Division.
Punjab: 4-day rain warning in West-Chandigarh, temperature to drop by 2C-3C in 48 hours
Rain and thunderstorms are warned for the next 4 days in Punjab and Chandigarh. A yellow alert has been issued here. Heavy rain occurred in Pathankot and Amritsar on Thursday. However, the temperature remained 3.8C higher than normal. The highest temperature of 33.5C was recorded in Sri Anandpur Sahib.
